title: A single-email verify is bulk-of-1 — read results[0], not the top level
summary: emailVerify normalizes every submission (one email or a hundred) to the same bulk envelope: {total, summary, billable_count, results[], metadata}. Even when you submitted a single `email`, the verdict is in results[0] — the legacy top-level email/status/score fields were removed because they were always None for single jobs. Agents that read result.status (top level) get nothing and wrongly report 'no result'. Always read results[0] for single, results[] for batch; trust summary for counts (it includes fuzziq_skipped, which results[] does not).
references: app/schemas/job.py:217-228 — SpiderVerifyData docstring: single is bulk-of-1, read results[0]
app/api/v1/jobs/transforms.py:328-368 — transform_spiderverify_data wraps single→bulk; legacy flat fields dropped
